source_context:
This procedure has three functional layers (stages) laid out left to right as three large colored containers connected by arrows.

LAYER 1 - Classification (yellow container, unit: post). Vertical flow of short-labeled nodes: Platform records -> Rule + keyword screen -> Candidate posts -> Relevance coding -> Dimension tagging -> Classified post base (emphasized box with dark border).

LAYER 2 - EB aggregation (blue container, unit: district x dimension). Vertical flow: Classified post base -> Dong linkage -> Exposure denominator -> Observed post rate -> EB shrinkage -> Posterior rate -> High-z_shift review cells (emphasized box). An arrow labeled "classified base" runs from Layer 1's Classified post base to the top of Layer 2.

LAYER 3 - Evidence-grounded judgment (green container, unit: post-level case). Five execution stages A-E split by an INFORMATION BOUNDARY drawn as two dashed sub-boxes side by side:
- Left dashed box I_R: Stage A/B isolated inputs. Stage A post record branches to three isolated hypothesis agents stacked vertically: Abductive hyp., Forward hyp., Sequential hyp. (Stage B).
- Right dashed box I_J: Stage C/D/E evidence set. Vertical flow: Stage C data specification -> Stage D rule-based evidence -> Stage E judgment -> Supported final coding.
- An arrow labeled "review cells" runs from Layer 2's High-z_shift review cells into Layer 3.
- Clean arrows run from the three hypothesis agents in I_R to Stage C in I_J, crossing the information boundary.

Keep node labels to 1-2 words. Minimize descriptive text inside the figure (no per-node sub-captions). The key meaning to preserve visually: hypothesis generation (I_R, Stage A/B) is isolated from EB divergence and district profiles, while only judgment (I_J, Stage C/D/E) sees expanded evidence.
communicative_intent: Three-layer UMC pipeline. Layer 1 (Classification, unit: post) screens and tags posts into a Classified post base. Layer 2 (EB aggregation, unit: district x dimension) links posts to dong, computes an exposure denominator and observed post rate, applies empirical Bayes shrinkage to a posterior rate, and flags High-z_shift review cells. Layer 3 (Evidence-grounded judgment, unit: post-level case) runs Stages A-E across an information boundary: restricted set I_R (Stage A/B hypothesis generation: Abductive, Forward, Sequential) is isolated from EB divergence and district profiles, while expanded set I_J (Stage C/D/E) grounds the Supported final coding in rule-based evidence.
